2606.26981.title: In-Context Model Predictive Generation: Open-Vocabulary Motion Synthesis from Language Models to Physics
2606.26981.paper_summary: Synthesizing human motion from textual descriptions is essential for immersive digital applications, yet existing methods face a persistent trade-off between semantic fidelity and physical realism. Large language model (LLM)-based approaches can interpret diverse open-vocabulary instructions and compose high-level action plans, but they often generate motions that violate physical constraints. Physics-aware models improve realism through simulation or control, but they struggle with semantic complexity, fine-grained instructions, and novel concepts. To address this gap, we propose In-Context Model Predictive Generation (ICMPG), a framework that integrates language-model planning with inference-time physical feedback. ICMPG reformulates motion synthesis as a Model Predictive Control (MPC)-like process with two modules. The Context-Aware Motion Generation (CAMG) module uses an LLM as a planner to decompose textual commands and generate candidate motion sequences from motion tokens. The Model Predictive Generation (MPG) module evaluates these candidates through physical simulation and semantic alignment, estimates a composite reward, and selects the best sequence to guide subsequent generation steps. Unlike open-loop generation, this closed-loop refinement enables ICMPG to adapt motions to both the input semantics and the simulated physical environment without task-specific policy retraining. Extensive experiments across standard and zero-shot open-vocabulary settings show that ICMPG generalizes robustly to diverse commands and produces motions that are more physically plausible and semantically faithful than representative baselines on the evaluated benchmarks. The framework bridges semantic interpretation and physical simulation while remaining flexible enough to incorporate different LLM backbones, enabling more versatile and controllable text-driven motion synthesis.
2606.26981.paper_summary_zh: 合成來自文本描述的人類動作對於沉浸式數位應用至關重要，但現有方法在語義真實性和物理真實性之間面臨持續的權衡。基於大型語言模型（LLM）的方法可以解釋多樣的開放詞彙指令並構建高層次的行動計劃，但它們往往生成違反物理約束的動作。具備物理意識的模型通過模擬或控制來提高真實感，但在語義複雜性、細緻指令和新概念方面面臨挑戰。為了填補這一空白，我們提出了上下文模型預測生成（ICMPG），這是一個將語言模型規劃與推理時物理反饋相結合的框架。ICMPG 將動作合成重新構造為類似模型預測控制（MPC）的過程，包含兩個模塊。上下文感知動作生成（CAMG）模塊使用 LLM 作為規劃者，將文本指令分解並從動作標記生成候選動作序列。模型預測生成（MPG）模塊通過物理模擬和語義對齊來評估這些候選者，估算綜合獎勵，並選擇最佳序列以指導後續生成步驟。與開放迴路生成不同，這種閉環精煉使 ICMPG 能夠將動作適應於輸入語義和模擬的物理環境，而無需針對特定任務的政策再訓練。在標準和零樣本開放詞彙設置下的廣泛實驗表明，ICMPG 能夠對多樣的指令進行穩健的泛化，並在評估基準上生成比代表性基準更具物理合理性和語義忠實性的動作。該框架在語義解釋和物理模擬之間架起了橋樑，同時保持足夠的靈活性以納入不同的 LLM 骨幹，實現更具多樣性和可控性的文本驅動動作合成。
2606.26981.author: Xiaomeng Fu et.al.
2606.26981.authors: Xiaomeng Fu, Junfan Lin, Yang Liu, Yaowei Wang, Guanbin Li, Liang Lin, Ziliang Chen
